title: Cenchrus longisetus
intro: Cenchrus longisetus, also known as Nigrospora, is a type of desert grass or perennial grass plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. The health benefits of Cenchrus longisetus include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cenchrus longisetus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cenchrus longisetus is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is commonly known as the long-spined burrgrass. This species is native to Africa and Asia. Cenchrus longisetus is a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which includes several species of grasses. It is often found in dry areas and has long, sharp spines that aid in seed dispersal.
uses: Cenchrus longisetus, also known as the long-hairy bur grass, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and effects of Cenchrus longisetus.

side_effects: Cenchrus longisetus, also known as the sandbur grass, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals upon contact. The plant's spines can also cause puncture wounds, leading to inflammation and discomfort. In rare cases, ingestion of Cenchrus longisetus can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting.
